articles.title: TEDIOUS FEEDBACK LOOPS
articles.summary: This Atlassian ebook [4] reveals how modern marketers can eliminate common campaign bottlenecks, with practical tips on how to:

articles.title: THIRD-PARTY SOURCES DRIVE 85% OF BRAND DISCOVERY
articles.summary: AI search mentions come mostly from third-party sources, accounting for 85% of all brand mentions. Only 13% originate from brand-owned domains. Brands are 6.5X more likely to be cited externally than on their own sites. Nearly 90% of these mentions appear in listicles, comparisons, or reviews. Visibility varies across AI platforms, with 68% of brands appearing on only one system. Owned content builds credibility, while third-party validation boosts recognition and discoverability in AI search.

articles.title: SECURE PREPAID TAGS COULD BOOST RETAILERS' CONFIDENCE IN GIFT CARDS
articles.summary: Infineon and Karta Gift Card have launched NFC-enabled prepaid tags with cryptographic protection. These tags eliminate printed barcodes and visible codes, replacing them with encrypted chips for tap-to-activate or redeem functionality. For brands that offer gift cards, this offers not just fraud mitigation but also a gateway to smarter CRM integration and post-purchase engagement.

articles.title: RETAIN YOUR CUSTOMER THE EASIER WAY USING AI
articles.summary: Brands using AI-driven retention models can reduce churn by 50-70% and increase customer lifetime value by up to 200%. AI can identify churn risk up to 60 days in advance and trigger targeted interventions by analyzing behavior data such as drops in feature usage or login frequency. Personalized retention strategies address specific causes like poor onboarding or price sensitivity, while lifetime value systems deepen engagement and loyalty. The guide includes a 3-step playbook with sample prompts.

articles.title: SHOULD YOU REMOVE EXCLUSION LISTS? (REAL TEST RESULTS)
articles.summary: Removing exclusion lists from Google Ads campaigns led to worse performance in a 2.5-month test. It showed lower conversions, higher CPAs, and reduced efficiency. The experiment found that 3 of 4 campaigns performed worse after all exclusions were removed. The results show that well-applied exclusions still help Smart Bidding by focusing on high-intent traffic. However, outdated or overly aggressive exclusions can hurt performance, so advertisers should reassess older lists and test before making changes.

articles.title: HOW CONTEXTUAL MESSAGING INCREASED PAID SUBSCRIBERS BY 9%
articles.summary: Adding contextual overlays to explain premium features increased Mobbin's paid subscribers by 9%. The new design used progressive disclosure, showing an overlay with visuals, feature descriptions, and an upgrade button when users hovered over “Pro” labels. This approach lets users understand the value of premium content without leaving their current task. During the test, 21% of visitors interacted with the overlay, showing strong interest in learning about Pro features at key moments.

articles.title: REACH MILLIONS OF TECH PROFESSIONALS AT SCALE
articles.summary: Over 6 million tech professionals read TLDR including developers, product managers, marketers, designers and executives. Get in front of your target audience with an ad placement just like this one! Learn more [10] about running a test campaign.

articles.title: PICTORY
articles.summary: Pictory turns scripts, blog posts, or long videos into short, shareable clips. It automatically summarizes content, selects visuals from a library of 18M clips and images, adds captions, voiceovers, and music, and lets users customize branding and themes. Features include text-to-video, URL-to-video, screen recording, and video highlight extraction. It requires no editing experience.

articles.title: 60 BLACK FRIDAY SMS TEMPLATES
articles.summary: This guide provides SMS templates designed to stay within the 160-character limit for various message types. It includes early access, abandoned cart reminders, and Black Friday promotions.

articles.title: TRUMP AND XI WILL ‘CONSUMMATE' TIKTOK DEAL ON THURSDAY
articles.summary: TikTok's US operations are moving to a new structure, with a board of American investors and Oracle managing platform security. The deal, set to be signed Thursday by Trump and Xi, also ends a series of extensions to force TikTok's sale. With control now based in the US, platform policies, ad systems, and data use may shift quickly.

articles.title: WHY IS IT SO HARD TO SHOW MARTECH'S PAYBACK?
articles.summary: Measuring martech ROI is challenging because organizations treat it as a one-time expense instead of a long-term capability. Tools often end up automating old processes rather than driving growth. To realize martech's potential, treat it as an evolving capability, link it to business outcomes like retention or revenue, and simplify the stack by consolidating overlapping systems.

articles.title: CRISIS OR COMEDY: WHEN SHOULD BRANDS LAUGH IT OFF?
articles.summary: Brands need to know when to use humor and when to stay serious. Humor can turn a scandal into an advantage, as shown by Astronomer's ad with Gwyneth Paltrow and Jet2's embrace of TikTok memes. However, it can also fail, like e.l.f.'s campaign with Matt Rife, which clashed with audience values. The key is to match tone with brand identity, respond quickly, and read the room. Well-timed humor can defuse tension, but misjudged jokes damage trust. The best responses show awareness, not spin.

articles.title: DOES SCHEMA MARKUP HELP AI VISIBILITY?
articles.summary: Schema markup may help organic rankings and AI visibility, but it's minor.

articles.title: AI IS DUMBER ON MONDAYS
articles.summary: AI quality can change silently when companies update or deploy buggy models.

articles.title: INSTAGRAM ADDS WATCH HISTORY FOR REELS
articles.summary: The new “Watch History” feature allows users to see any Reel viewed in the past 30 days.

articles.title: THE BEST MARKETING IDEAS FROM THE PAST 7 DAYS
articles.summary: Viral tactics include keyboard smashes, blurred reveals, and a crane brand's perfect moment.
